evals.prompt: My R01 budget period ends this summer and I need to put together the annual RPPR in eRA Commons. Can you help me draft the Accomplishments and Products sections from my progress notes and publication list?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-grant-reporting in NIH-RPPR mode: identifies this as an Annual RPPR and drafts it in the funder's own section order using the verified A-I skeleton — here Section B (Accomplishments: major goals, what was accomplished, results, dissemination, plans for next period) and Section C (Products: publications, datasets, software, IP). Ties each accomplishment back to a stated aim, notes that Section H (Budget) applies on non-SNAP annual RPPRs, and marks any missing figure or publication detail as a [TODO] placeholder rather than inventing it. Notes the user submits through eRA Commons.

assertions.value: Drafts the report in NIH's own RPPR section order (does not merge or rename sections) and leaves [TODO] placeholders instead of fabricating publications or numbers.

evals.prompt: My NSF award is wrapping up. I need to file the final project report and the Project Outcomes Report in Research.gov. What's the difference between them and can you draft the public outcomes summary?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-grant-reporting in NSF mode: explains that the final project report refers only to the final funded year (not cumulative) while the Project Outcomes Report is a separate public-audience summary of no more than 800 words, both submitted in Research.gov within 120 days after the award end date, and drafts the POR for a general audience under the 800-word cap. Does not conflate the two reports.

assertions.value: Distinguishes the final project report (final funded year only) from the public 800-word Project Outcomes Report and keeps the POR within the word cap.

evals.prompt: I coordinate a Horizon Europe project and our first reporting period just ended. I need to prepare the periodic report on the Funding & Tenders Portal — help me structure the technical narrative and figure out the deadline.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-grant-reporting in Horizon Europe / ERC mode: explains the report is a Technical Report (Part A structured tables generated by the Portal from the Continuous Reporting module, Part B narrative that mirrors the application form and reports deviations) plus a Financial Report (Financial Statements), and computes the 60-days-after-period-end deadline (optionally via scripts/report_deadlines.py --funder eu). Tells the user it is submitted on the EU Funding & Tenders Portal and to confirm against the Grant Agreement.

assertions.value: Describes the Horizon Europe Part A / Part B technical report plus Financial Statements and gives the 60-day periodic-report deadline.

evals.prompt: We under-spent on personnel because our postdoc started four months late, and now we won't finish data collection in time. I need to request a no-cost extension and write the budget variance explanation for the report. Can you draft both?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-grant-reporting in NCE / budget-variance mode: writes a budget-vs-actual narrative for Personnel (planned vs actual, the late-hire reason, the effect on the affected aim/milestone, and the carry-forward action) and an NCE request structured as programmatic justification + unobligated balance + revised timeline + explicit confirmation that no new scope and no new funds are requested. Marks unknown amounts as [TODO] and tells the user to confirm extension limits/prior-approval against the Notice of Award.

assertions.value: Produces a category-level variance narrative AND a no-cost-extension justification that explicitly states no new scope or funds are requested, with [TODO] placeholders for unknown figures.

evals.prompt: I'm putting together a brand-new NSF proposal. Can you help me write the project description and broader-impacts section and build the budget for the submission?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-research-grants. The user is writing a NEW proposal (pre-award) — project description, broader impacts, and a fresh budget for submission — not reporting against an award already made. alterlab-grant-reporting only handles post-award deliverables (progress/final reports, variance narratives, NCEs).

evals.prompt: TÜBİTAK 1001 çağrısına başvuracağım. Yeni projenin özgün değer ve yöntem bölümlerini ve bütçesini hazırlamama yardım eder misin?
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke this skill; defers to alterlab-tubitak-proposal. The user is preparing a NEW TÜBİTAK 1001 proposal (pre-award, Turkish funder) — özgün değer, yöntem, and budget for submission — not a post-award progress or final report. alterlab-grant-reporting handles post-award reporting, not Turkish proposal authoring.
